title: Tinus sphenobasis
taxonomy: Tinus sphenobasis is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Achariaceae. It is native to tropical Africa and belongs to the genus Tinus. Within the Achariaceae family, Tinus sphenobasis is further categorized as a member of the order Malpighiales. The classification of Tinus sphenobasis is based on its morphological characteristics and phylogenetic relationships with other plants. Further research may be necessary to determine the exact classification and relationships within the plant kingdom.

The leaves and roots of the plant are believed to possess antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be used to treat various ailments. benefits: Tinus sphenobasis, also known as the Dwarf Mondo Grass, has several reported health benefits.
